ai.chat.index.748794-108: The auto-detect hit rate is used to observe whether default auto-detection is stable. It is not the same as manually labeled accuracy.
ai.chat.index.748794-109: The manual mode change rate is used to observe whether advanced mode often needs to be switched.
ai.chat.index.748794-110: The correction success rate only counts the "Retry with Correction" event in the result panel.
ai.chat.index.748794-111: The default path uses auto-detect. Specify manually only for temporary correction or when the whole session should use a fixed ability.

ai.chat.index.748794-118: If not specified, the current session strategy will be used
ai.chat.index.748794-121: AI session quality observability for the current account
ai.chat.index.748794-122: Only standard send requests are counted, excluding clarification reruns. This helps observe auto-detect stability, manual correction frequency, and convergence after correction.
ai.chat.index.748794-124: The auto-detect hit rate is currently calculated as "AUTO requests without a result-panel correction retry". It is a proxy metric for product stability, not manually labeled accuracy.
ai.chat.index.748794-125: The manual mode change rate only counts standard send requests, including pinned sessions, this-round overrides, and direct legacy-side assignments. Clarification reruns are excluded.
ai.chat.index.748794-126: The correction success rate only counts the "Retry with Correction" event in the result panel. Historical messages without events before this release are not included in the numerator or denominator.

ai.chat.index.748794-360: AI Session is the unified intelligent entry in the platform. It can automatically identify whether the customer is asking about platform operations, business data, device runtime values, device control, protocol parsing, thing model generation, or preliminary evaluation from requirement documents.

ai.model.index.748794-25: Only local runtime model snapshot cache statistics are shown, for observing hit rate, TTL, and active invalidation.
ai.model.index.748794-32: These are local runtime snapshot cache statistics for the current node and do not represent future cluster-wide totals in multi-instance deployments.
ai.model.index.748794-33: The hit rate is calculated as hits divided by actual cache lookup checks and excludes bypass requests when caching is disabled.
ai.model.index.748794-34: Cold miss means there is no local cache entry yet. Expired rebuild means the entry was hit but its TTL has expired.
ai.model.index.748794-36: Common parameters can be selected directly. Custom keys are also supported; values support numbers, booleans, JSON arrays, and objects.
ai.model.index.748794-48: Cache is disabled. Current requests bypass it directly.
ai.model.index.748794-50: Indicates the number of runtime snapshot entries still retained in the current node
ai.model.index.748794-52: The former is cold misses; the latter is rebuilds after TTL expiration.
